title: poinsettia cuphosperma
intro: Poinsettia cuphosperma, also known as Mexican firecracker, is a species of flowering plant in the poinsettia genus. It is native to Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, as well as supporting immune function and reducing oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Poinsettia cuphosperma can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of ailments.
